title: Add 'you are headless' rule to all stage command prompts
description: In test runs 3-4, stage agents that hit contradictory constraints ended their turn by asking the nonexistent human a question ('Two ways forward, your call') instead of reporting blocked in the status block; run 3's planner lost its entire plan text that way because only the final message survives a stage. Add an explicit rule to every stage command file (commands/PLAN.md, IMPLEMENT.md, TEST.md, REVIEW.md, and PRIME.md if it gives behavioral instructions): you are running headless, nobody will ever answer a question, and any contradiction or blocker must be reported as outcome=blocked in the structured status block with the reason in failure_reason. Evidence: observability/test_run1.md follow-up 2.
acceptance_criteria: Each of commands/PLAN.md, commands/IMPLEMENT.md, commands/TEST.md, commands/REVIEW.md contains an explicit headless rule: no human will answer questions, and contradictions/blockers must be reported as outcome=blocked in the status block instead of asking
The rule names the status block as the only channel for blockers (failure_reason carries the why)
A pytest test asserts the headless rule text is present in all four stage command files so it cannot silently regress
Full suite uv run pytest -q stays green
